text: fat is 0.4g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.
carbs is 6.6g. glycemic index, sugar, and starch are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
protein is 2.8g. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
vitamin k at 85% DV, vitamin c at 99% DV, vitamin a at 12% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side. very strong micronutrient payoff because likely to challenge spinach for elite vegetable status. good fibre support because solid maintenance-and-repair fit. strong cruciferous reputation because viewed as a flagship healthy vegetable.
the drawbacks next. taste can be polarising because not universally loved. prep matters a lot because overcooking hurts it badly. can be rough on digestion for some because a practical downside.
A tier. Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
